Margin & Liquidation Rules | Manage Risk on PowerTrade

  • Account Health & Liquidation | Thresholds & Stages: How account health is calculated and what triggers reduce‑only, ADL and forced liquidation.
  • Portfolio Margin (SPAN) | Scenarios & Shock Factors: SPAN‑based scenario margining using spot and volatility shocks; nine PV scenarios in USD.
  • Margin Methodology | Inputs, Scenarios & Parameters: USD‑denominated portfolio margin with IV surface, nine scenarios and pair‑level aggregation.

Account Health & Liquidation | Thresholds & Stages

Account Health

The PowerTrade platform estimates and displays your account's health rating on the portfolio screen. This estimate is an indicator of how risky the portfolio is. Once certain risk thresholds are crossed, the account could be taken over by our auto-deleveraging system, or could even undergo liquidation.

If you find your account health number getting low, depositing more assets is the fastest way to avoid having your account deleveraged.

Formula

Account health is displayed as a percentage value between 0 and 100. The formula used for calculating account health is as follows:

Health = Available / Assets * 100

where

Assets    = Spot assets and pending settlements

Available = Assets + Unrealized - Margin

For more information on Margin calculation, please refer to this article.

SPAN Margin Liquidation Stages

State Threshold Description
Healthy >25% Account is not at risk
At Risk 25% Account is at risk and goes into reduce only mode. Orders that increase maintenance margin not allowed. Withdrawals disabled.
Auto-Deleverage 5% Auto-hedger takes over account and attempts to close positions or delta hedge until account goes below threshold.
Instant Liquidation 0% Assets liquidated at market price until account goes below threshold.
Bankrupt 0% Account is bankrupt.

Still have questions?

Email Support: support@power.trade

Telegram: https://t.me/power_trade

Margin Methodology | Inputs, Scenarios & Parameters

Power Trade margin is denominated in USD. For the purposes of accounting, the exchange assumes a constant USDC/USD rate of 1.0

The Margin engine applies SPAN methodology to the entire portfolio, with PV measured in USD terms.

Methodology

Margin requirement is computed periodically using real-time data. At discrete moments in time, the system samples spot, futures and options mark prices.

Funding costs are implied from futures and spot marks.

An implied volatility surface is generated for each pair where options are supported, using observed options marks, spot marks and implied funding costs.

Each currency pair is considered in turn. For each pair, a theoretical PV is computed for all positions that have this pair as the underlying currency. This includes spot, futures, perpetuals and options. The PV is computed in terms of USD.

For each currency pair, the inventory of non-usd coins in the customer’s wallet is treated as USD converted at the current mark price plus a long synthetic overnight future. In this way, non-usd currencies are considered as margin cover in USD while providing the full benefit of covered call positions.

The PV of each currency-pair set is computed for each of nine scenarios, where inputs are the Spot Shock Factor (SSF) and Volatility Shock Factor (VSF). The Implied Volatility (IV) is interpolated for each product from the computed volatility surface using the tenor of the option and the Spot Rate Input (SRI).

It is important to note that the treatment of non-usd coins is only considered if there are held positions in the customer’s account that are derivatives of the given underlying non-usd coin. In other words simply holding a non-usd coin (like ETH) does not result in an automatic haircut being applied to the value of the coin, and any treatment is capped by the impact of exposure resulting from those held positions.

For example, if the value of a held position is significantly smaller than the value held of the underlying, then a scenario which sees a value at risk of 15% of the held position which is itself only 4% of the value of the underlying, means that the scenario analysis will cap the “impact” of holding the underlying to that 4% and not assume a value at risk of 15% as expected for the scenario in play.

Definitions

SSF - Spot Shock Factor VSF - Volatility Shock Factor

IV - Implied Volatility for a given tenor and moneyness SRI - Spot Rate Input

Scenarios

Scenario Number Spot Rate Input Implied Volatility Input
1 Spot Mark IV
2 Spot Mark IV + (IV * VSF)
3 Spot Mark IV - (IV * VSF)
4 Spot Mark * (1 + SSF) IV
5 Spot Mark * (1 + SSF) IV + (IV * VSF)
6 Spot Mark * (1 + SSF) IV - (IV * VSF)
7 Spot Mark * (1 - SSF) IV
8 Spot Mark * (1 - SSF) IV + (IV * VSF)
9 Spot Mark * (1 - SSF) IV - (IV * VSF)

Note that both the volatility shock and the spot shock are additive.

Once the nine PVs are computed, the scenario with the lowest PV is selected.

This process is repeated for each currency pair and the resulting PV of each selected scenario is accumulated.

The margin requirement is the difference between the accumulated scenario PV and the accumulated PV of the un-shocked portfolio.

Margin requirement is always stated in USD.

The margin system has the capability to set a correlation between currency pairs. At the present time, this capability is not enabled.

Configurable Parameters

The input parameters are:

  • Spot Shock Factor (SSF)
  • Volatility Shock Factor (VSF)

Parameters may be modified from time to time in response to adverse market conditions. However, the baseline parameters are as follows:

ETH BTC SOL AVAX
SSF 15% 15% 15% 15%
VSF 40% 40% 40% 40%

PowerTrade offers a Testnet where you could enter options and outright positions to see the margin calculations for a portfolio. https://powertrade-web-test.web.app

Main Account / Sub accounts Margin

The PowerTrade Margin engine calculates the margin required for each (sub)account.

To benefit from margin offset, futures and options contracts should be traded in the same (sub)account.

If two different strategies are traded via two sub-accounts, each sub-account is margined separately.

Still have questions?

Email Support: support@power.trade

Telegram: https://t.me/power_trade

Portfolio Margin (SPAN) | Scenarios & Shock Factors

For Portfolio Margin, PowerTrade uses the SPAN Margin method, there are some choices to be made in terms of methodology and parameters when bumping implied volatility. These choices impact the scenarios which are considered in order to assess the health risk and hence liquidation of a client's portfolio.

Methodology

The Margin requirement is computed periodically using real-time data. At 100 ms intervals the margin engine samples spot, futures, perpetuals and options mark prices.

An implied volatility surface is generated for each pair where options are supported, using observed options marks, spot marks and funding costs.

For each pair, a theoretical PV is computed for all positions that have this pair as the underlying currency. This includes spot, futures, perpetuals and options. The PV is computed in terms of USD.

For each currency pair, the inventory of non-USD coins in the customer’s wallet is treated as USD converted at the current mark price . In this way, non-USD currencies are considered as margin cover in USD.

The PV of each currency-pair set is computed for each of nine scenarios, where inputs are the Spot Shock Factor (SSF) and Volatility Shock Factor (VSF)

Configurable Parameters

The input parameters are:

  • Spot Shock Factor (SSF)
  • Volatility Shock Factor (VSF)

Definitions

SSF - Spot Shock Factor, configurable per asset and account,

the default value = 0.15;

VSF - Volatility Shock Factor, configurable per asset and account,

the default value = 0.40 x (45 / days till expiry)^0.3;

IV - Implied Volatility for a given tenor and moneyness

SRI - Spot Rate Input

Parameters may be modified from time to time in response to adverse market conditions. However, the baseline parameters are as follows:

ETHBTCSOLAVAX
SSF15%15%15%15%
VSF40%40%40%40%

PowerTrade can change the risk settings without prior notice.

Portfolio Scenarios

The margin engine calculates 9 scenarios to calculate the margin requirement.

Scenario NumberSpot Rate InputImplied Volatility Input
1max(0, Spot Mark * (1 + SSF))max(0, IV * (1 + VSF))
2max(0, Spot Mark * (1 + SSF)IV
3max(0, Spot Mark * (1 + SSF)max(0, IV * (1 - VSF))
4Spot Markmax(0, IV * (1 + VSF))
5Spot MarkIV
6Spot Markmax(0, IV * (1 - VSF))
7max(0, Spot Mark * (1 - SSF))max(0, IV * (1 + VSF))
8max(0, Spot Mark * (1 - SSF))IV
9max(0, Spot Mark * (1 - SSF))max(0, IV * (1 - VSF))

Note that both the volatility shock and the spot shock are additive.

Once the nine PVs are computed, the scenario with the lowest PV is selected.

This process is repeated for each currency pair and the resulting PV of each selected scenario is accumulated.

The margin requirement is the difference between the accumulated scenario PV and the accumulated PV of the un-shocked portfolio. Margin requirement is always stated in USD.

The margin system has the capability to set a correlation between currency pairs. At the present time, this capability is not enabled.

Main Account / Sub Accounts Margin

The PowerTrade Margin engine calculates the margin required for each (sub)account.

Clients can benefit from a margin offset by trading, futures and options contracts in the same (sub)account. Different positions in sub-accounts will be margined separately.

Testnet

PowerTrade offers a Testnet (https://powertrade-web-test.web.app) where you could enter options and outright positions to simulate different portfolios.

Still have questions?

Email Support: support@power.trade

Telegram: https://t.me/power_trade

PowerTrade Options Contract Specifications | BTC, ETH & Altcoins

European-Style USDC-Settled Options

PowerTrade offers European-style crypto options across BTC, ETH, and 80+ altcoin markets. All contracts are cash-settled in USDC, ensuring simplicity, transparency, and reduced custody risk.

Key Features:

  • Exercise Style: European-style — options can only be exercised at expiry, not before. This prevents early exercise risk and helps traders focus purely on market moves up to the expiration date.
  • Settlement: Cash-settled in USDC, so you never need to hold the underlying cryptocurrency.
  • Pricing: All options are quoted in USD terms, based on blended exchange rate sources.
  • Trading Hours: 24/7, giving traders around-the-clock access to crypto derivatives.

What Are European-Style Options?

European-style options differ from American-style options in a key way:

  • European Options: Can only be exercised on the expiration date. This makes them ideal for traders who want a clear and risk-defined payoff structure, without the complexity of early exercise.
  • American Options: Can be exercised at any time before expiry, which can add flexibility but also complicates risk management and pricing.

For crypto traders, European-style options offer:

  • ✅ Simpler strategies (no unexpected early exercise)
  • ✅ Cleaner risk management with predictable settlement
  • ✅ Focus on expiry-based trading — ideal for hedging or speculating on key events (e.g., rate decisions, token unlocks, or ETF approvals)

Why Trade Options on PowerTrade?

  • ✅ European-style, USDC-settled — simple, transparent, and risk-defined
  • ✅ Access to BTC, ETH & 80+ altcoin options markets (the widest coverage in crypto)
  • ✅ 24/7 global trading access
  • ✅ Transparent margining and settlement process
  • ✅ Flexible expiries — from daily to 3 months forward

Still have questions?

📩 Email Support: support@power.trade

💬 Telegram 24/7 Desk: https://t.me/power_trade

ADA Options Trading – Contract Specs, Strategies & Market Insights on PowerTrade

📄 ADA Options Trading – Contract Specs, Strategies & Market Insights on PowerTrade

Cardano (ADA) is one of the most widely adopted Layer 1 blockchains, and with ADA options listed on PowerTrade, traders can hedge, speculate, or capture volatility with precision. With expirations up to 12 months, institutional RFQ execution, and retail-friendly order book access, ADA options are a flexible tool for both long-term investors and active traders.

👉 Start trading ADA options on PowerTrade or PowerDEX.

What Are ADA Options?

ADA options are derivatives that give the holder the right, but not the obligation, to buy or sell Cardano at a set strike price before expiry.

  • ADA Calls → Profit from upside moves in ADA’s price.
  • ADA Puts → Hedge downside exposure or trade bearish scenarios.

Options provide defined downside risk and flexibility beyond spot or perpetuals.

Expiration Dates Available

PowerTrade lists ADA options with 8 expirations, covering both short-term and long-term maturities of up to 12 months.

  • Short-term ADA options → Trade daily or weekly volatility.
  • Longer-term ADA options → Ideal for hedging staking exposure or positioning for ecosystem growth.

BTC Options Trading – Contract Specs, DEGEN Options & Strategies on PowerTrade

Bitcoin is the world’s largest cryptocurrency, and BTC options are the cornerstone of crypto derivatives trading. On PowerTrade, traders gain access to expirations from 0DTE up to 12 months, DEGEN ultra-short contracts, RFQ block trading, and deep strike coverage — giving both retail and institutional traders the most flexible way to trade BTC volatility.

👉 Start trading BTC options on PowerTrade or PowerDEX.

What Are BTC Options?

BTC options are financial derivatives that give you the right but not the obligation to buy or sell Bitcoin at a chosen strike price before expiry.

  • Call options → Bullish positioning on Bitcoin.
  • Put options → Hedge or profit from potential downside.

Options give traders the ability to hedge spot BTC holdings, trade volatility, or build structured strategies with defined downside — unlike perpetuals, where forced liquidations are a constant risk.

Expiration Dates Available

PowerTrade lists BTC options with expirations from 0DTE (same day) up to 12 months forward.

  • Short-dated BTC options (daily / weekly) → great for momentum plays or event-driven trading (CPI, Fed decisions, ETF flows).
  • Longer-dated contracts (quarterly / yearly) → used for institutional hedging and structured strategies.

This wide coverage makes BTC options far more versatile than perpetual swaps.

  • No liquidation risk — unlike perps, your downside is fixed at premium paid.

📄 ETC Options Trading – Contract Specs, Strategies & Market Insights on PowerTrade

Ethereum Classic ($ETC) is the original Ethereum blockchain, maintained after the DAO fork in 2016. It is secured by proof-of-work (PoW) and has a strong following as a “sound money” smart contract platform. With ETC options on PowerTrade, traders can hedge, speculate, and capture volatility in one of the most historic Layer 1 blockchains. PowerTrade offers expirations up to 12 months, institutional RFQ block-trading, and retail-friendly order book execution.

👉 Start trading ETC options on PowerTrade or PowerDEX.

What Are ETC Options?

ETC options are derivatives that grant the right, but not the obligation, to buy or sell Ethereum Classic at a chosen strike price before expiry.

  • ETC Calls → Express bullish bets on ETC adoption and PoW sustainability.
  • ETC Puts → Hedge exposure or speculate on downside risks.

Unlike perpetuals, ETC options provide defined downside risk while enabling complex multi-leg strategies.

Expiration Dates Available

PowerTrade lists ETC options with 8 expirations, spanning 0DTE (same-day expiry) to 12 months forward.

  • Short-term ETC options → Useful for trading volatility around upgrades or macro sentiment.
  • Long-term ETC options → Hedge holdings or speculate on the sustained role of PoW smart contract chains.

ETH Options Trading – Contract Specs, DEGEN Options & Strategies on PowerTrade

Ethereum is the backbone of DeFi, and ETH options are the most liquid altcoin derivatives in crypto. On PowerTrade, traders gain access to expirations from 0DTE up to 12 months, short-term DEGEN contracts, and advanced RFQ trading for institutional block trades.

👉 Start trading ETH options on PowerTrade or PowerDEX.

What Are ETH Options?

ETH options are financial derivatives that give you the right but not the obligation to buy or sell Ethereum at a chosen strike price before expiry.

  • Call options → Bullish positioning.
  • Put options → Downside hedging.

Options provide defined-risk exposure and enable strategies not possible with spot or perps.

Expiration Dates Available

PowerTrade offers ETH options with expirations from 0DTE to 12 months.

  • Short-dated ETH options → Trade around events (Fed meetings, ETF flows).
  • Longer-dated ETH options → Hedge portfolios or express macro views.

SOL Options Trading – Contract Specs, DEGEN Options & Strategies on PowerTrade

Looking to trade SOL options with flexibility and speed? PowerTrade lists multiple expirations, deep strike coverage, and DEGEN 10-minute & 1-hour contracts for traders who want to capture volatility on Solana.

👉 Start trading SOL options on PowerTrade or PowerDEX.

What Are SOL Options?

SOL options are derivatives that give you the right but not the obligation to buy or sell Solana (SOL) at a chosen strike price before expiry.

  • Call options → Bet on rising SOL prices.
  • Put options → Protect against or profit from downside moves.

Options give traders the ability to hedge, speculate, and trade volatility with defined downside risk — unlike perpetuals, where sudden moves can lead to liquidation.

Expiration Dates Available

PowerTrade offers SOL options with expirations ranging from same-day (0DTE) up to 12 months.

  • Short-dated contracts (daily / weekly) → trade momentum and quick price swings.
  • Longer-dated contracts (up to 1 year) → hedge portfolio risk or build strategic positions.

This wide range of expiries makes SOL options more versatile than any perpetual or spot market.

Options Introduction | European, USDC‑Settled Crypto Options

Learn about European style, USDC cash-settled crypto options for Bitcoin, Ethereum and 80 altcoin markets offered on PowerTrade.

PowerTrade Crypto Options: Trade BTC, ETH & 80+ Altcoins

PowerTrade is the leading platform for European-style, USDC cash-settled crypto options, offering Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), and over 80 altcoin options markets. Whether you want to hedge, speculate, or build advanced strategies, PowerTrade provides the most flexible and capital-efficient tools in the crypto derivatives market.

What Are Crypto Options?

Crypto options are financial derivatives that give traders the right, but not the obligation, to buy or sell an underlying cryptocurrency at a fixed price (the strike price) on or before a specific date.

Unlike spot trading or perpetual futures, options allow you to limit your downside while keeping unlimited upside potential. On PowerTrade, all options are European-style and settled in USDC, giving you direct, transparent exposure to crypto price movements without holding the underlying asset.

Premiums

The premium is the cost you pay to buy an option. It depends on factors like:

  • Strike price
  • Time to expiration
  • Current market price of the underlying crypto
  • Market volatility

On PowerTrade, all option premiums are denominated in USDC, making them simple and transparent.

Call Options

A call option gives the buyer the right (not the obligation) to buy the cryptocurrency at the strike price.

  • Traders buy calls when they expect the crypto price to rise.
  • Profit potential is unlimited if the price moves above the strike price.

Example: A trader buys a BTC call option with a $50,000 strike price. If BTC rises to $55,000 before expiry, the trader can buy at $50,000 and capture the $5,000 difference (minus the premium).

Put Options

A put option gives the buyer the right (not the obligation) to sell the cryptocurrency at the strike price.

  • Traders buy puts when they expect the crypto price to fall.
  • Useful for hedging against downside risk.

Example: A trader buys an ETH put option with a $3,000 strike price. If ETH drops to $2,500, the trader can sell at $3,000, profiting $500 (minus the premium).

European Style Options

PowerTrade offers European-style options, which can only be exercised at the expiration date. This structure:

  • Prevents early exercise risk
  • Simplifies strategy planning
  • Keeps the focus on price moves up to expiry

USDC Cash-Settled Options

All PowerTrade options are cash-settled in USDC:

  • No need to hold BTC, ETH, or altcoins directly
  • Settlement in stable USDC makes it easy to manage profits and losses
  • Reduces custody risk compared to physically settled options

Why Trade Options on PowerTrade?

  • ✅ 80+ altcoin options markets (the most in crypto)
  • ✅ Tight pricing & deep liquidity
  • ✅ Risk-defined strategies with capped downside
  • ✅ Flexible expirations — daily to 3 months forward
  • ✅ Simple settlement in USDC

PowerTrade gives you the ultimate platform to trade, hedge, and profit from volatility in the crypto markets.

Get Started Today

📩 Email Support: support@power.trade
💬 Telegram 24/7 Desk: https://t.me/power_trade

👉 Start Trading Options on PowerTrade or PowerDEX

  • PowerTrade Perpetual Funding Rate Methodology (Migration Overview): Learn how PowerTrade calculates perpetual funding rates with industry-standard precision. This guide explains the legacy and new Bybit-aligned methodology, impact margin notional, funding formula

Full Perpetual Documentation | Mechanics & Examples

PowerTrade Perpetuals are a margin product that allows users to take a view on a crypto asset price (BTC, ETH, etc.) and profit on the asset’s appreciation or depreciation without having to hold the actual crypto asset. If a user believes the price of the asset will increase, the user can take a “long” view by opening a long position. The user will make a profit if the price appreciates above the entry price (plus any costs such as trading fees). The entry price is the price of the asset (also known as the mark price) estimated by the system at the time the trade was entered into. Alternatively, if the user believes the asset’s price will go down, they can enter into a “short” position and profit if the price moves below the entry price (minus any fees paid to enter into the trade).

PowerTrade Perpetuals settle in cash (USD) and do not require the user to hold or deliver the actual asset. Perpetuals are similar to Financial Futures in several aspects: (1) they represent a contract between two parties based on the future price of an asset where one party (the buyer) pays the other party (the seller) at the end of the term depending on whether there is price appreciation or vice versa , (2) they settle every period of time (every 1 hour in the case of PowerTrade Perpetuals) at the end of which cash flows are exchanged and profit may be settled.

PowerTrade Perpetuals are different from financial Futures in that (1) there is no set term or expiration and they continue in perpetuity and (2) a cash flow swap takes place at the end of the 1 hour term. The amount swapped is determined by the swap rate, which is calculated and applied every second as described in the table below.

Cash flows in one direction: either accounts with long positions pay accounts with short positions or vice versa, depending on whether the swap rate is a positive or negative value. This mechanism ensures that prices do not diverge too far from the underlying spot index.

The table below shows specific characteristics and attributes of PowerTrade Perpetuals.

Priority = PnL Percentage * Effective Leverage

IF PnL percentage > 0 Priority = PnL Percentage / Effective Leverage

IF PnL percentage < 0 Effective Leverage = Mark Value / abs (Avg. Entry Value - Bankrupt Value)

PnL Percentage = abs (Mark Value - Avg. Entry Value) / Avg. Entry Value

Mark Value = Position Value at Mark Price

Bankrupt Value = Position Value at Bankruptcy Price

Avg. Entry Value = Position Value at Avg. Entry Price

Perpetuals Contract Specifications | Mark, Funding & PnL

Overview

PowerTrade Perpetuals are advanced crypto derivatives that let traders take long or short positions on digital assets (e.g., BTC, ETH) without holding the underlying tokens.
This section details the contract specifications, mark price logic, funding mechanism, and PnL calculation for PowerTrade’s perpetual futures.

📘 For a detailed explanation of how PowerTrade calculates funding rates, mark prices, and impact notional depth, see the full Funding Rate Methodology page.

Introduction to Perpetuals

Perpetual futures are margin-based trading instruments that track the price of an underlying asset without an expiry date.

Long Position

Traders open a long position if they expect the asset price to rise.
Profit = (Mark Price – Entry Price) × Position Size.

Short Position

Traders open a short position if they expect the asset price to fall.
Profit = (Entry Price – Mark Price) × Position Size.

Difference Between Perpetuals and Term Futures

Unlike traditional term futures, PowerTrade Perpetuals have no expiry date.
They automatically roll over and remain open indefinitely. Traders gain or lose depending on price movements and periodic funding payments, which keep contract prices close to the underlying spot market.

BTC/USD Perpetual Futures Specifications

Parameter Description
Underlying Index BTC-USD-INDEX as published by PowerTrade
Contract Size 1 Contract = 1 BTC
Minimum Tick Size 0.50
Minimum Quantity Step 0.0001
Initial Margin 20%
Maintenance Margin Portfolio Margined (SPAN methodology)
Funding Currency USD
Settlement Frequency Every 1 hour
PnL Currency USD

Mark Price Calculation

PowerTrade uses a Mark Price to fairly determine unrealized PnL and trigger liquidations.
It is derived from real-time market data and a short-term exponential moving average to smooth volatility.

Reference Price = Last traded price or manually set price.

Market Price = Reference Price
if it falls between the best bid and best ask;
otherwise:
- Use best bid if Reference < best bid
- Use best ask if Reference > best ask

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price - Index Price)

The Exponential Moving Average (EMA) is calculated over 15 seconds to ensure price stability.

Funding Rate & Swap Logic

Funding payments keep perpetual prices aligned with the spot market.
They are exchanged between long and short traders once per hour based on the calculated Swap Rate.

Formula

Swap Rate = Premium Rate + Differential Interest Rate

Premium Rate

MIS = (Mark Price - Index Price) / Index Price
&#xNAN;Premium Rate = Max(0.05%, MIS) + Min(-0.05%, MIS)

Differential Interest Rate

Differential Interest Rate = Interest(USD) - Interest(BTC)

(Currently set to 0.)

Interpretation:

  • If Swap Rate > 0, longs pay shorts.
  • If Swap Rate < 0, shorts pay longs.

The swap is accrued continuously in unrealized PnL and settled hourly between counterparties.

Funding Settlement Change (Q1 2026)

As part of PowerTrade’s new funding model rollout:

  • Funding will only apply to open positions at settlement time.
  • Positions closed before the hourly funding timestamp will not pay or receive funding.

This change aligns PowerTrade’s logic with Bybit’s settlement system and creates a fairer, more predictable experience for traders.

📘 Learn more in our Funding Rate Methodology.

Frequency of Funding Calculation

Funding is computed continuously but applied every hour.
Because the Swap Rate is expressed as a daily percentage, each second’s rate is divided by 86,400 (seconds in a day) for precise accrual.

Profit & Loss (PnL)

Profit or Loss (P&L) for an open position is determined using the Mark Price and Entry Price.

Long Position

P&L = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price) + settled funding

Short Position

P&L = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price) + settled funding

PnL becomes realized once the position is closed.

Liquidation Logic

If the Account Balance + P&L ≤ Maintenance Margin, the system initiates liquidation:

  1. Cancel all open orders.
  2. If balance remains below requirement, positions are force-closed at market to restore margin.

Insurance & Auto-Deleveraging (ADL)

  • Insurance Fund: Covers losses if a trader’s account goes negative.
  • Auto-Deleveraging: If the insurance fund is insufficient, profitable counterparties’ positions are reduced proportionally based on leverage and PnL priority.

Priority formula:

Priority = PnL% × Effective Leverage (if PnL% > 0)
&#xNAN;Priority = PnL% / Effective Leverage (if PnL% < 0)

ETH/USD Perpetual Futures Specifications

Parameter Description
Underlying Index ETH-USD-INDEX as published by PowerTrade
Contract Size 1 Contract = 1 ETH
Minimum Tick Size 0.05
Minimum Quantity Step 0.001
Initial Margin 20%
Maintenance Margin Portfolio Margined (SPAN methodology)
Funding Currency USD
Settlement Frequency Every 1 hour
PnL Currency USD

Mark price, funding logic, and liquidation rules follow the same structure as BTC perpetuals.

Transparency & Reproducibility

All PowerTrade perpetual data — including mark prices, index prices, and historical funding rates — can be independently verified using published APIs and historical data.

For a full explanation of PowerTrade’s hourly funding rate mechanism, impact margin notional tiers, and reproducibility guide, visit the
👉 PowerTrade Funding Rate Methodology.

PowerTrade — Transparent, fair, and data-driven crypto derivatives.

Still have questions?

Email Support: support@power.trade

Telegram: https://t.me/power_trade

AAVE Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade – Funding Rate, Contract Specs & Trading Guide

Trade AAVE perpetual futures on PowerTrade with zero fees, hourly settlements, and transparent, benchmark-aligned funding rates.
AAVE perpetuals give traders the flexibility to speculate or hedge AAVE price movements without owning the underlying asset — offering deep liquidity, multi-collateral margining, and fair funding model.

👉 Start trading AAVE Perpetual Futures now on PowerTrade or on its decentralized counterpart Polaris.

What Are AAVE Perpetual Futures?

AAVE perpetual futures are derivatives that let traders go long or short on AAVE with leverage, without ever holding the underlying token.

  • Long AAVE Perpetual: Profit when AAVE’s price rises.
  • Short AAVE Perpetual: Profit when AAVE’s price declines.

Unlike dated futures, perpetual contracts never expire. Instead they use funding payments exchanged between longs and shorts to maintain price alignment with the AAVE spot market.

AAVE Perpetual Contract Specifications

Parameter Details
Instrument Name AAVE-PERPETUAL
Underlying Asset AAVE (Governance Token of Aave Protocol)
Settlement Currency USDC
Leverage Up to 100×
Settlement Frequency Hourly
Trading Fees 0% maker / taker
Collateral Options BTC, ETH, and stablecoins
Margin Mode Isolated or Portfolio Margin

PowerTrade perpetuals are designed for both retail and institutional traders, featuring ultra-low latency, predictable funding, and transparent margining.

AAVE Perpetual Funding Rate Explained

PowerTrade’s AAVE funding rate keeps perpetual prices in line with the AAVE index by using a benchmark-based formula derived from real-time market data and a global AAVE pricing reference.

Funding Rate Formula:

F = P + clamp(I – P, +0.05%, –0.05%)

Where:

  • P (Premium Index): Difference between market and index price.
  • I (Interest Rate): 0.01% per 8 hours (prorated hourly).
  • Clamp: Limits changes to ±0.05% to avoid distortions.

Funding is applied hourly and only affects positions open at settlement.
Traders who close positions before the funding timestamp pay no funding fees.

👉 Learn more: PowerTrade Funding Rate Calculation Guide

AAVE/USDC Index Price Methodology

The AAVE/USDC Index Price represents the fair and real-time market value of AAVE against USDC, calculated from aggregated spot prices.
📌 Reference Source: AAVE on CoinGecko CoinGecko+1

Purpose of the Index

The Index Price serves as the reference price for AAVE perpetual contracts, ensuring that mark prices and liquidation levels reflect the broader market rather than the activity of a single exchange.
This design helps maintain market integrity, prevents price manipulation, and improves fairness in derivatives pricing.

Index Construction

  • The index is derived from a weighted average of spot AAVE/USDC (or similar pairs) prices collected from multiple qualified exchanges.
  • Each reference exchange’s contribution is based on:
    • Data reliability and uptime history
    • Trading volume and liquidity depth
    • Price consistency relative to the global market
  • Prices that fail data-quality checks (e.g., stale, delayed, or extreme outliers) are automatically excluded from the calculation.
  • Only validated sources are used in the final weighted index to ensure high accuracy and resistance to manipulation.

Calculation and Updates

  • The index is calculated and published every second (or near real‐time) to provide up-to-date pricing.
  • A weighted average formula is applied to combine all validated prices.
  • The final Index Price reflects the consensus value of AAVE/USDC across global markets.

Fallback Mechanism

If spot market data becomes temporarily unavailable or unreliable, the system will switch to a pre-market index calculation using bid and ask prices from the perpetual contract order book itself.
This ensures continuous pricing even during exchange outages or data disruptions.
During such periods, external spot exchange prices will no longer affect the Index Price.

Mark Price and Fair Liquidations

PowerTrade’s mark price mechanism ensures fair PnL and liquidation processes:

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price – Index Price)

Where:

  • The EMA (Exponential Moving Average) is computed over a short interval (e.g., 15 seconds) to smooth market volatility.
  • This ensures PowerTrade’s AAVE perpetuals reflect real market conditions while minimizing the risk of unfair liquidations or manipulation.

PnL and Liquidation Logic

Long AAVE Position:
PnL = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price)
Short AAVE Position:
PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidations occur when:
Account Balance + Unrealised PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

When liquidation is triggered:

  • All open orders are cancelled.
  • Positions are closed at market.
  • The insurance fund and auto-deleveraging (ADL) mechanisms protect other traders and maintain market stability.

Why Trade AAVE Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade?

✅ 0% Trading Fees – Trade AAVE perpetuals with zero maker or taker fees.
✅ Hourly Settlements – Faster than traditional 8-hour or multi-hour funding cycles.
✅ Benchmark-Aligned Funding – Transparent and reproducible formula tied to AAVE index pricing.
✅ Multi-Collateral Margining – Use BTC, ETH, or stablecoins for flexibility.
✅ Portfolio Margin – Cross-margin between perpetuals and options.
✅ Trade Anywhere – Available on PowerTrade (CEX) and Polaris (DEX).

PowerTrade’s AAVE perpetual market delivers deep liquidity, transparent funding, and institutional-grade performance, ideal for both professionals and active traders.

Example AAVE Perpetual Use Cases

1️⃣ Hedge Spot AAVE Holdings
If you hold AAVE tokens and anticipate a downturn, you can short AAVE perpetuals to hedge exposure.

2️⃣ Leverage Trading
Want to amplify your directional view on AAVE price movements? Use leverage with full control of hourly funding.
3️⃣ Funding Rate Arbitrage
Exploit funding rate differentials for AAVE perpetuals across exchanges and between spot holdings and futures.
4️⃣ Short-Term Volatility Plays
Take advantage of short-term swings in AAVE’s price with leverage, zero fees, and predictable funding costs.

Funding Transparency and Reproducibility

PowerTrade stands out as the only exchange where funding rates are fully reproducible from public order-book and index data.
Every rate is independently verifiable — reinforcing transparency and fairness.

👉 Check the full methodology: PowerTrade Perpetual Funding Rate Methodology

How to Start Trading AAVE Perpetuals

  1. Create an account on PowerTrade or Polaris.
  2. Deposit collateral — USDC, ETH, or BTC.
  3. Select AAVE-PERPETUAL in the perpetual markets tab.
  4. Trade instantly with 0% fees and hourly funding transparency.

Conclusion

PowerTrade’s AAVE Perpetual Futures deliver maximum efficiency, full transparency, and zero trading fees — empowering traders to speculate, hedge, or arbitrage with precision.
Whether you’re hedging AAVE exposure, capturing funding yield, or trading price swings, PowerTrade and Polaris provide the most advanced, fair, and liquid AAVE perpetual markets in crypto.

ADA Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade – Funding Rate, Contract Specs & Trading Guide

Trade ADA perpetual futures on PowerTrade with zero fees, hourly settlements, and benchmark-aligned funding rates based on the CoinGecko ADA/USDC Index.
ADA perpetuals let traders speculate or hedge Cardano price movements with leverage — featuring multi-collateral margining, transparent funding, and deep liquidity.

👉 Start trading ADA Perpetual Futures now on PowerTrade or its decentralized counterpart Polaris.

What Are ADA Perpetual Futures?

ADA perpetual futures are derivatives that allow traders to go long or short on Cardano (ADA) price movements — without holding the underlying asset.

  • Long ADA Perpetual: Profit when ADA price rises.
  • Short ADA Perpetual: Profit when ADA price falls.

Unlike traditional futures, ADA perpetuals never expire. Instead, a funding rate mechanism between long and short positions keeps prices aligned with the ADA spot market.

ADA Perpetual Contract Specifications

Parameter Details
Instrument Name ADA-PERPETUAL
Underlying Asset Cardano (ADA)
Settlement Currency USDC
Leverage Up to 100×
Settlement Frequency Hourly
Trading Fees 0% maker / taker
Collateral Options BTC, ETH, and stablecoins
Margin Mode Isolated or Portfolio Margin

PowerTrade perpetuals combine institutional-grade infrastructure with transparent funding and multi-collateral flexibility, designed for both professional and retail traders.

ADA Perpetual Funding Rate Explained

The AERO/USDC Index ensures PowerTrade’s ADA perpetual prices stay in line with the broader ADA market by referencing CoinGecko’s ADA/USDC Index.

Funding Rate Formula:

F = P + clamp(I – P, +0.05%, –0.05%)

Where:

  • P (Premium Index): Market price deviation from index price.
  • I (Interest Rate): 0.01% per 8 hours (prorated hourly).
  • Clamp: Limits adjustment to ±0.05% to maintain stability.

Funding is applied hourly, only to positions open at settlement.
Close your position before the funding timestamp to avoid funding fees.

👉 Learn more: PowerTrade Funding Rate Calculation Guide

ADA/USDC Index Price Methodology

The ADA/USDC Index Price tracks Cardano’s fair market value using spot prices aggregated from CoinGecko’s global exchange feed.
📊 Reference Source: Cardano (ADA) on CoinGecko

Purpose of the Index

The Index Price ensures fair and transparent pricing for ADA perpetual contracts — preventing manipulation from isolated market activity and ensuring mark prices and liquidations reflect the global ADA market.

Calculation

PowerTrade integrates CoinGecko’s weighted spot index, which averages prices across qualified exchanges based on:

  • Liquidity and volume weight
  • Price consistency
  • Exchange reliability

CoinGecko filters out stale or outlier data to produce a robust and manipulation-resistant ADA price benchmark.
The ADA Index updates every second to maintain real-time accuracy.

Fallback Mechanism

If external data becomes unavailable, PowerTrade temporarily switches to a pre-market internal index, derived from order book bid/ask data for ADA perpetuals.
This guarantees continuous mark pricing, even during external data disruptions.

Mark Price and Liquidation Logic

PowerTrade’s mark price ensures fair PnL and liquidation levels:

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price – Index Price)
  • The EMA (Exponential Moving Average) is calculated over a 15-second window to smooth volatility.
  • This approach minimizes liquidation risk during price spikes and ensures ADA perpetuals remain fairly marked.

PnL Formulas:

  • Long ADA Position: PnL = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price)
  • Short ADA Position: PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidation triggers when:
Account Balance + Unrealized PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

When liquidation occurs:

  1. All open orders are canceled.
  2. Positions are closed at market.
  3. The insurance fund and ADL system ensure market stability.

Why Trade ADA Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade?

✅ 0% Trading Fees – Zero maker/taker fees across all perpetuals.
✅ Hourly Settlements – Funding recalculated and applied every hour.
✅ Benchmark-Aligned Funding – Based on the transparent CoinGecko ADA index.
✅ Multi-Collateral Margining – Trade using BTC, ETH, or stablecoins.
✅ Portfolio Margin – Cross-margin ADA perpetuals with other positions.
✅ Trade Anywhere – On PowerTrade (CEX) or Polaris (DEX).

PowerTrade’s ADA perpetuals offer deep liquidity, verifiable funding, and fair pricing — ideal for both active traders and institutional hedgers.

Protect your Cardano spot holdings by shorting ADA perpetuals.

2️⃣ Leverage Trading
Go long or short ADA with up to 100× leverage for amplified exposure.

3️⃣ Funding Rate Arbitrage
Capture differences between PowerTrade’s and other exchanges’ funding rates.

Profit from short-term ADA price swings using leverage and transparent funding.

Funding formulas are reproducible, ensuring every trader can audit the fairness of the system.

What Are AERO Perpetual Futures?

AERO perpetual futures are derivatives that allow traders to go long or short on Aerodrome Finance (AERO) price movements — without ever holding the underlying token.

  • Long AERO Perpetual: Profit when AERO price rises.
  • Short AERO Perpetual: Profit when AERO price declines.

Unlike dated futures, perpetual contracts have no expiration date. Instead, they rely on funding payments exchanged between longs and shorts to keep contract prices aligned with AERO’s spot market value.

AERO Perpetual Contract Specifications

Parameter Details
Instrument Name AERO-PERPETUAL
Underlying Asset Aerodrome Finance (AERO)
Settlement Currency USDC
Leverage Up to 100x
Settlement Frequency Hourly
Trading Fees 0% maker / taker
Collateral Options BTC, ETH, and stablecoins
Margin Mode Isolated or Portfolio Margin

PowerTrade perpetuals combine ultra-low latency, fair funding, and cross-margin flexibility — ideal for both retail and institutional traders.

AERO Perpetual Funding Rate Explained

The PowerTrade AERO funding rate ensures that the perpetual contract price remains aligned with the AERO/USDC index price derived from CoinGecko.

Funding Rate Formula:

F = P + clamp(I – P, +0.05%, –0.05%)

Where:

  • P (Premium Index): Difference between market and index price.
  • I (Interest Rate): 0.01% per 8 hours (prorated hourly).
  • Clamp: Restricts adjustment to ±0.05% to prevent funding spikes.

Funding is applied hourly, and only open positions at settlement are affected.
Traders who close positions before the funding timestamp pay no funding fees.

👉 Learn more: PowerTrade Funding Rate Calculation Guide

AEROUSDC Index Price Methodology

The AERO/USDC Index reflects the fair market value of Aerodrome Finance against USDC, based on aggregated spot market data from CoinGecko.

📊 Reference Source: AERO on CoinGecko

Purpose of the Index

The index price acts as the reference price for AERO perpetual contracts — ensuring mark prices and liquidation levels mirror the true global market value of AERO, independent of local exchange activity.

This protects traders against price manipulation and ensures fair and transparent contract pricing.

Calculation

PowerTrade sources real-time AERO/USDC data from CoinGecko’s composite market feed.
CoinGecko’s index aggregates spot prices from leading exchanges, filtering out unreliable or stale quotes to produce a consistent benchmark.

The final AERO Index Price represents the weighted average market value across trusted global markets and is updated every second.

Fallback Mechanism

If CoinGecko’s feed becomes temporarily unavailable, PowerTrade uses a pre-market index calculated from bid/ask prices in the AERO perpetual order book.
This guarantees continuous pricing and protects open positions during data interruptions.

Mark Price and Liquidation Logic

To ensure fairness and transparency, PowerTrade uses a mark price that smooths short-term volatility:

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price – Index Price)
  • The EMA (Exponential Moving Average) is calculated over 15 seconds.
  • This reduces unnecessary liquidations and ensures mark pricing reflects true market value.

PnL Calculations:

  • Long AERO Position:
    PnL = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price)
  • Short AERO Position:
    PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidations occur when:
Account Balance + Unrealized PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

When liquidation is triggered:

  1. Open orders are canceled.
  2. Positions are closed at market.
  3. The insurance fund and ADL mechanisms protect counterparties and maintain system stability.

Why Trade AERO Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade?

✅ 0% Trading Fees – Zero maker/taker fees on all perpetuals.
✅ Hourly Settlements – Faster than standard 8-hour funding cycles.
✅ Transparent Funding – Formula aligned with public benchmarks.
✅ Multi-Collateral Margining – Use BTC, ETH, or stablecoins as collateral.
✅ Portfolio Margin – Cross-margin perpetuals and options.
✅ Trade Anywhere – Available on PowerTrade (CEX) and Polaris (DEX).

PowerTrade’s AERO perpetuals deliver deep liquidity, reproducible funding rates, and institutional-grade performance.

Example AERO Perpetual Use Cases

1️⃣ Hedge AERO Holdings
Protect spot AERO exposure by shorting AERO perpetuals.

2️⃣ Leverage Trading
Amplify directional exposure on AERO with hourly-settled control.

3️⃣ Funding Rate Arbitrage
Exploit funding differentials between PowerTrade and other exchanges.

4️⃣ Volatility Speculation
Trade short-term AERO price swings using leverage and 0% fees.

Every funding rate is reproducible — promoting transparency and fairness across all AERO perpetual markets.

AEVO Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade – Funding Rate, Contract Specs & Trading Guide

Trade AEVO perpetual futures on PowerTrade with zero fees, hourly settlements, and transparent, benchmark-aligned funding rates powered by the CoinGecko AEVO/USDC Index.
AEVO perpetuals enable traders to speculate or hedge Aevo’s price movements with leverage, multi-collateral margining, and fair funding transparency.

👉 Start trading AEVO Perpetual Futures now on PowerTrade or its decentralized counterpart Polaris.

What Are AEVO Perpetual Futures?

AEVO perpetual futures are crypto derivatives that let traders take long or short positions on Aevo (AEVO) without owning the underlying token.

  • Long AEVO Perpetual: Profit when AEVO’s price increases.
  • Short AEVO Perpetual: Profit when AEVO’s price decreases.

Unlike traditional dated futures, AEVO perpetuals never expire. Instead, a funding rate mechanism between longs and shorts maintains price alignment with AEVO’s global spot market.

AEVO Perpetual Contract Specifications

Parameter Details
Instrument Name AEVO-PERPETUAL
Underlying Asset Aevo (AEVO)
Settlement Currency USDC
Leverage Up to 100×
Settlement Frequency Hourly
Trading Fees 0% maker / taker
Collateral Options BTC, ETH, and stablecoins
Margin Mode Isolated or Portfolio Margin

PowerTrade perpetuals combine institutional-grade execution, zero-fee trading, and multi-collateral margining — designed for both professional and retail traders.

AEVO Perpetual Funding Rate Explained

PowerTrade’s AEVO funding rate model ensures perpetual prices track the CoinGecko AEVO/USDC Index, reflecting AEVO’s true market value.

Funding Rate Formula:

F = P + clamp(I – P, +0.05%, –0.05%)

Where:

  • P (Premium Index): Difference between the contract price and index price.
  • I (Interest Rate): 0.01% per 8 hours (applied hourly).
  • Clamp: Caps adjustment to ±0.05% to prevent funding spikes.

Funding applies hourly only to positions open at settlement.
Positions closed before the funding timestamp incur no funding costs.

👉 Learn more: PowerTrade Funding Rate Calculation Guide

AEVO/USDC Index Price Methodology

The AEVO/USDC Index Price reflects the fair global market value of Aevo (AEVO) against USDC, using real-time pricing data from CoinGecko.

Purpose of the Index

The index price acts as the reference rate for AEVO perpetual contracts, ensuring mark prices and liquidations represent the global consensus price, not the behavior of a single venue.

This maintains price integrity and protects traders from exchange-specific volatility.

Index Construction

CoinGecko’s composite index aggregates spot AEVO/USDC prices from multiple qualified exchanges, weighting them by:

  • Trading volume and liquidity depth
  • Data reliability and uptime
  • Price alignment with global market averages

Outlier or stale data points are automatically excluded.
PowerTrade uses this validated composite feed to anchor AEVO perpetual mark prices in real time.

Fallback Mechanism

If CoinGecko data becomes unavailable, PowerTrade automatically switches to a pre-market internal index derived from bid/ask data on the AEVO-PERPETUAL order book.
This ensures uninterrupted pricing and reliable mark-to-market calculations.

📊 Reference Source: AEVO on CoinGecko

Mark Price and Liquidation Logic

PowerTrade’s mark price smooths short-term volatility to ensure fair and consistent liquidations.

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price – Index Price)
  • EMA (Exponential Moving Average) over a 15-second window smooths volatility.
  • Prevents unfair liquidations and maintains consistent mark pricing.

PnL Calculations:

  • Long AEVO: PnL = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price)
  • Short AEVO: PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidation condition:
Account Balance + Unrealized PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

Upon liquidation:

  1. Open orders are canceled.
  2. Positions close at market price.
  3. Insurance fund and ADL systems absorb losses, protecting counterparties.

Why Trade AEVO Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade?

✅ 0% Trading Fees – No maker/taker fees.
✅ Hourly Funding Settlements – Faster and more accurate than 8-hour cycles.
✅ Transparent Funding Model – Based on reproducible CoinGecko index data.
✅ Multi-Collateral Margining – Use BTC, ETH, or stablecoins.
✅ Portfolio Margin – Cross-margin AEVO perpetuals with other assets.
✅ Trade Anywhere – On PowerTrade (CEX) or Polaris (DEX).

PowerTrade’s AEVO perpetuals provide deep liquidity, auditable funding rates, and zero-fee execution — ideal for professionals and active traders alike.

Protect spot AEVO positions by shorting AEVO perpetuals.

2️⃣ Leverage Trading
Amplify AEVO exposure with leverage and precise hourly settlements.

3️⃣ Funding Arbitrage
Capture funding rate differences across global exchanges.

Trade AEVO’s short-term price moves with leverage and 0% fees.

This guarantees transparency, fairness, and data integrity.

  2. Deposit collateral – USDC, BTC, or ETH.
  3. Select AEVO-PERPETUAL from the markets list.
  4. Trade instantly with 0% fees and hourly funding transparency.

ALGO Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade – Funding Rate, Contract Specs & Trading Guide

Trade ALGO perpetual futures on PowerTrade with zero fees, hourly settlements, and benchmark-aligned funding rates powered by the CoinGecko ALGO/USDC Index.

What Are ALGO Perpetual Futures?

ALGO perpetual futures are derivatives that allow traders to take long or short positions on Algorand (ALGO) price movements — without ever owning the underlying asset.

  • Long ALGO Perpetual: Profit when ALGO’s price increases.
  • Short ALGO Perpetual: Profit when ALGO’s price decreases.

Unlike traditional dated futures, perpetuals never expire. Instead, they use funding payments exchanged between long and short positions to keep prices aligned with ALGO’s global spot market.

ALGO Perpetual Contract Specifications

Parameter Details
Instrument Name ALGO-PERPETUAL
Underlying Asset Algorand (ALGO)
Settlement Currency USDC
Leverage Up to 100×
Settlement Frequency Hourly
Trading Fees 0% maker / taker
Collateral Options BTC, ETH, and stablecoins
Margin Mode Isolated or Portfolio Margin

PowerTrade perpetuals are built for speed, transparency, and fairness, featuring multi-collateral support, zero-fee trading, and institutional-grade infrastructure.

ALGO Perpetual Funding Rate Explained

PowerTrade’s ALGO funding rate ensures that the perpetual contract price stays in line with Algorand’s spot market, using benchmark data from CoinGecko.

Funding Rate Formula:

F = P + clamp(I – P, +0.05%, –0.05%)

Where:

  • P (Premium Index): Difference between the perpetual market price and index price.
  • I (Interest Rate): 0.01% per 8 hours (applied hourly).
  • Clamp: Restricts change to ±0.05% to prevent excessive funding moves.

Funding is applied hourly and only affects positions open at settlement.
Traders who close before the funding timestamp avoid funding costs.

👉 Learn more: PowerTrade Funding Rate Calculation Guide

ALGO/USDC Index Price Methodology

The ALGO/USDC Index represents Algorand’s fair market value against USDC, using aggregated real-time data from CoinGecko’s composite market feed.

📊 Reference Source: ALGO on CoinGecko

Purpose of the Index

The index price acts as the reference rate for ALGO perpetual contracts.
It ensures mark prices and liquidations are based on the global average ALGO price rather than any single exchange’s fluctuations — maintaining fairness and preventing manipulation.

Index Construction

The index price is derived from a weighted average of ALGO/USDC (or equivalent) spot pairs across multiple major exchanges.
Each exchange’s contribution depends on:

  • Liquidity and 24-hour trading volume
  • Historical uptime and data reliability
  • Price consistency vs. global averages

CoinGecko’s algorithm automatically excludes stale or outlier prices to maintain high accuracy and resistance to manipulation.
PowerTrade then applies this validated CoinGecko price feed to calculate ALGO mark and index prices.

Fallback Mechanism

If CoinGecko’s data feed becomes unavailable, PowerTrade temporarily switches to a pre-market internal index using order book bid/ask data for the ALGO perpetual contract.
This ensures uninterrupted mark pricing and reliable liquidations even during external data outages.

Mark Price and Liquidation Logic

PowerTrade uses a mark price mechanism that ensures smooth and fair PnL calculations:

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price – Index Price)
  • The EMA (Exponential Moving Average) over a 15-second window filters out short-term volatility.
  • This mechanism ensures fair liquidation thresholds and prevents unnecessary liquidations.

PnL Formulas:

  • Long ALGO: PnL = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price)
  • Short ALGO: PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidation Trigger:
Account Balance + Unrealized PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

Upon liquidation:

  1. All open orders are cancelled.
  2. Positions are closed at market price.
  3. The insurance fund and ADL (auto-deleveraging) systems maintain platform stability.

Why Trade ALGO Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade?

✅ 0% Trading Fees – Zero maker or taker fees for perpetual trading.
✅ Hourly Funding – Faster and more accurate than 8-hour settlement cycles.
✅ Transparent Funding Model – Derived from public CoinGecko data.
✅ Multi-Collateral Margining – Trade with BTC, ETH, or stablecoins.
✅ Portfolio Margin – Combine positions across perpetuals and options.
✅ Trade Anywhere – On PowerTrade (CEX) or Polaris (DEX).

APE Perpetual Funding Rate Explained

APE/USDC Index Price Methodology

The APE/USDC Index reflects ApeCoin’s fair market value against USDC, based on aggregated spot market data from CoinGecko.

📊 Reference Source: ApeCoin (APE) on CoinGecko

Purpose of the Index

The index price ensures fair pricing for APE perpetual contracts by anchoring them to the global market consensus rather than the volatility of a single venue.
This prevents manipulation, ensures fair mark pricing, and maintains market integrity.

Index Construction

The CoinGecko composite index aggregates APE/USDC prices across top global exchanges. Each source is weighted by:

  • 24-hour trading volume and liquidity
  • Data reliability and uptime
  • Price stability and deviation tolerance

CoinGecko automatically excludes outlier or stale prices to ensure precision and resistance to manipulation.
PowerTrade then applies this validated feed to mark and index pricing.

Fallback Mechanism

This guarantees uninterrupted pricing and accurate mark values even during data outages.

Mark Price and Liquidation Logic

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price – Index Price)
  • The EMA (Exponential Moving Average) is calculated over 15 seconds to smooth price volatility.
  • This reduces liquidation risk and ensures stability during high-volatility periods.

PnL Formulas:

  • Long APE: PnL = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price)
  • Short APE: PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidation Trigger:
Account Balance + Unrealized PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

When triggered:

  1. Open orders are canceled.
  2. Positions close at market price.
  3. The insurance fund and ADL systems absorb losses and maintain stability.

  • Short APT: PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidation Trigger:
Account Balance + Unrealized PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

Upon liquidation:

  1. All open orders are canceled.
  2. Positions are closed at market.
  3. PowerTrade’s insurance fund and ADL mechanisms protect counterparties and maintain fairness.

Why Trade APT Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade?

✅ 0% Trading Fees – No maker or taker fees.
✅ Hourly Funding – Updated and applied hourly for precision.
✅ Transparent Index Pricing – Based on CoinGecko APT market data.
✅ Multi-Collateral Margining – Use BTC, ETH, or stablecoins.
✅ Portfolio Margin – Cross-margin APT with other perpetuals and options.
✅ Trade Anywhere – On PowerTrade (CEX) or Polaris (DEX).

PowerTrade’s APT perpetuals provide deep liquidity, auditable funding rates, and institutional-grade performance.

Protect your Aptos spot position by shorting APT perpetuals.

Amplify APT exposure with up to 100× leverage for directional bets.

Profit from funding differences across multiple exchanges.

ARB Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade – Funding Rate, Contract Specs & Trading Guide

Trade ARB perpetual futures on PowerTrade with zero fees, hourly settlements, and benchmark-aligned funding rates based on the CoinGecko ARB/USDC Index.
ARB perpetuals let traders speculate or hedge Arbitrum price movements using leverage, multi-collateral margining, and transparent hourly funding.

👉 Start trading ARB Perpetual Futures now on PowerTrade or its decentralized counterpart Polaris.

What Are ARB Perpetual Futures?

ARB perpetual futures are leveraged derivative contracts that allow traders to take long or short positions on the price of Arbitrum (ARB) without owning the underlying token.

  • Long ARB Perpetual: Profit when ARB price rises.
  • Short ARB Perpetual: Profit when ARB price falls.

Unlike dated futures, ARB perpetuals never expire. Instead, they rely on funding payments exchanged between long and short traders to keep contract prices aligned with the ARB spot market.

ARB Perpetual Contract Specifications

Parameter Details
Instrument Name ARB-PERPETUAL
Underlying Asset Arbitrum (ARB)
Settlement Currency USDC
Leverage Up to 100×
Settlement Frequency Hourly
Trading Fees 0% maker / taker
Collateral Options BTC, ETH, and stablecoins
Margin Mode Isolated or Portfolio Margin

PowerTrade perpetuals are built for speed, capital efficiency, and transparency, offering zero-fee trading, benchmark-based pricing, and deep liquidity.

ARB Perpetual Funding Rate Explained

PowerTrade’s ARB funding rate mechanism ensures perpetual prices remain closely aligned with the ARB/USDC index derived from CoinGecko.

Funding Rate Formula:

F = P + clamp(I – P, +0.05%, –0.05%)

Where:

  • P (Premium Index): Difference between perpetual market price and index price.
  • I (Interest Rate): 0.01% per 8 hours (applied hourly).
  • Clamp: Restricts change to ±0.05% to prevent funding volatility.

Funding is applied hourly and only affects positions open at settlement.
Traders who close their positions before the funding timestamp avoid funding fees.

👉 Learn more: PowerTrade Funding Rate Calculation Guide

ARB/USDC Index Price Methodology

The ARB/USDC Index tracks Arbitrum’s fair market value against USDC using aggregated real-time data from CoinGecko’s composite feed.

📊 Reference Source: Arbitrum (ARB) on CoinGecko

Purpose of the Index

The index ensures mark prices and liquidation thresholds reflect the true global market value of ARB — rather than activity on a single exchange.
This design promotes fairness, transparency, and market integrity.

Index Construction

The index is derived from a weighted average of ARB/USDC (or equivalent) spot prices across major exchanges.
Each source’s weight is determined by:

  • Trading volume and liquidity
  • Historical reliability and uptime
  • Price stability versus global averages

CoinGecko automatically excludes stale or outlier data to ensure consistency.
PowerTrade uses this validated feed to calculate mark prices and funding rates.

Fallback Mechanism

If CoinGecko’s data feed becomes unavailable, PowerTrade switches to an internal pre-market index, derived from bid/ask data in the ARB perpetual order book.
This ensures continuous pricing and fair liquidations even during data disruptions.

Mark Price and Liquidation Logic

PowerTrade’s mark price mechanism ensures fair and transparent PnL calculations and liquidation processes.

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price – Index Price)
  • The EMA (Exponential Moving Average) is computed over a 15-second window to smooth short-term volatility.
  • This protects traders from unnecessary liquidations and ensures accurate mark-to-market pricing.

PnL Calculations:

  • Long ARB: PnL = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price)
  • Short ARB: PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidation Trigger:
Account Balance + Unrealized PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

When triggered:

  1. All open orders are canceled.
  2. Positions close at market price.
  3. The insurance fund and auto-deleveraging (ADL) system protect other traders from loss propagation.

Why Trade ARB Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade?

✅ 0% Trading Fees – Trade with zero maker or taker fees.
✅ Hourly Funding – Updated hourly for precise rate adjustments.
✅ Benchmark-Aligned Pricing – CoinGecko-based ARB index ensures accuracy.
✅ Multi-Collateral Margining – Use BTC, ETH, or stablecoins as margin.
✅ Portfolio Margin – Cross-margin with other perpetuals and options.
✅ Trade Anywhere – On PowerTrade (CEX) or Polaris (DEX).

PowerTrade’s ARB perpetual markets combine fair funding, deep liquidity, and cross-platform access, giving traders total control and efficiency.

Example ARB Perpetual Use Cases

1️⃣ Hedge ARB Spot Exposure
Short ARB perpetuals to protect your spot holdings during volatility.

2️⃣ Leverage Trading
Take directional positions with up to 100× leverage.

3️⃣ Funding Rate Arbitrage
Capture profit opportunities from ARB funding rate differentials.

4️⃣ Volatility Speculation
Trade ARB’s short-term price movements with transparent hourly funding and zero fees.

Funding Transparency and Reproducibility

PowerTrade offers complete funding transparency — every rate is reproducible using public index and order-book data.
This ensures that all traders operate on a level, verifiable system.

👉 See details: PowerTrade Perpetual Funding Rate Methodology

How to Start Trading ARB Perpetuals

  1. Create an account on PowerTrade or Polaris.
  2. Deposit collateral – USDC, BTC, or ETH.
  3. Select ARB-PERPETUAL from the perpetuals list.
  4. Start trading instantly with 0% fees and hourly funding transparency.

Conclusion

PowerTrade’s ARB Perpetual Futures deliver unmatched transparency, capital efficiency, and zero trading fees — allowing traders to hedge, speculate, or arbitrage Arbitrum price movements with precision.
Whether you’re trading ARB volatility or building long-term strategies, PowerTrade and Polaris provide the industry’s most transparent and liquid perpetual markets.

  • Short AR Perpetual: Profit when AR price falls.

Unlike traditional futures, perpetuals never expire. Instead, they use funding payments exchanged between longs and shorts to maintain alignment with the spot AR market price.

AR Perpetual Contract Specifications

Parameter Details
Instrument Name AR-PERPETUAL
Underlying Asset Arweave (AR)
Settlement Currency USDC
Leverage Up to 100×
Settlement Frequency Hourly
Trading Fees 0% maker / taker
Collateral Options BTC, ETH, and stablecoins
Margin Mode Isolated or Portfolio Margin

AR Perpetual Funding Rate Explained

The PowerTrade AR funding rate ensures AR perpetual prices stay aligned with the CoinGecko AR/USDC Index, reflecting the true market value of Arweave.

Funding Rate Formula:

F = P + clamp(I – P, +0.05%, –0.05%)

Where:

  • P (Premium Index): The difference between market and index prices.
  • I (Interest Rate): 0.01% per 8 hours (applied hourly).
  • Clamp: Restricts adjustment to ±0.05% to prevent extreme spikes.

Funding is applied hourly and affects only positions open at settlement.
Traders who close positions before funding time don’t pay funding fees.

👉 Learn more: PowerTrade Funding Rate Calculation Guide

AR/USDC Index Price Methodology

The AR/USDC Index tracks Arweave’s fair market value in USDC, using real-time data aggregated from multiple exchanges through CoinGecko’s composite feed.

📊 Reference Source: Arweave (AR) on CoinGecko

Purpose of the Index

The index ensures mark prices and liquidation levels reflect the global consensus price for AR, protecting traders from exchange-specific volatility or manipulation.

Index Construction

The CoinGecko composite index aggregates spot AR/USDC (or equivalent) prices across top global exchanges. Each venue’s contribution is weighted by:

  • Trading volume and liquidity depth
  • Exchange reliability and uptime
  • Consistency with the global average

Outlier and stale data are automatically filtered out.
PowerTrade then applies this validated CoinGecko feed to derive mark and index prices used across the platform.

Fallback Mechanism

If CoinGecko’s feed is temporarily unavailable, PowerTrade switches to an internal pre-market index based on AR perpetual order book bid/ask data, ensuring uninterrupted pricing and liquidation accuracy.

Mark Price and Liquidation Logic

PowerTrade’s mark price mechanism smooths volatility and ensures fair PnL and liquidation calculations.

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price – Index Price)
  • The EMA (Exponential Moving Average), calculated over 15 seconds, filters out short-term noise.
  • This reduces unnecessary liquidations and keeps pricing consistent during volatile periods.

PnL Formulas:

  • Long AR: PnL = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price)
  • Short AR: PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidation Trigger:
Account Balance + Unrealized PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

When triggered:

  1. Open orders are canceled.
  2. Positions close at market.
  3. The insurance fund and auto-deleveraging (ADL) mechanisms maintain system stability.

ATH/USDC Index Price Methodology

The ATH/USDC Index represents the fair, real-time market value of Aethir (ATH) against USDC, using aggregated spot data from CoinGecko’s composite exchange feed.

📊 Reference Source: Aethir (ATH) on CoinGecko

Purpose of the Index

The index acts as the reference rate for ATH perpetual contracts, ensuring fair pricing and protecting traders from single-exchange volatility.
It’s used to determine mark prices, liquidation levels, and funding calculations.

Index Construction

CoinGecko’s composite index aggregates ATH/USDC (or equivalent pairs) from multiple reputable exchanges and weights them by:

  • Trading volume and liquidity depth
  • Historical uptime and data reliability
  • Consistency with global market averages

CoinGecko automatically excludes stale or anomalous data to maintain index integrity.
PowerTrade integrates this validated feed to ensure accurate and manipulation-resistant pricing.

Fallback Mechanism

If CoinGecko’s data feed becomes unavailable, PowerTrade switches to an internal pre-market index using bid/ask data from the ATH perpetual order book.

Mark Price and Liquidation Logic

PowerTrade’s mark price mechanism ensures consistent and fair PnL calculations across all perpetual markets.

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price – Index Price)
  • The EMA (Exponential Moving Average) is calculated over a 15-second window to smooth out volatility.
  • This mechanism minimizes unfair liquidations and ensures stability during fast price swings.

PnL Formulas:

  • Long ATH: PnL = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price)
  • Short ATH: PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidation Trigger:
Account Balance + Unrealized PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

Upon liquidation:

  1. All open orders are canceled.
  2. Positions are closed at market.
  3. PowerTrade’s insurance fund and auto-deleveraging (ADL) system safeguard the market and other traders.

ATOM/USDC Index Price Methodology

AXL Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade – Funding Rate, Contract Specs & Trading Guide

Trade AXL perpetual futures on PowerTrade with zero fees, hourly settlements, and benchmark-aligned funding rates powered by the CoinGecko AXL/USDC Index.
AXL perpetuals let traders speculate or hedge Axelar price movements with leverage, multi-collateral margining, and transparent hourly funding.

👉 Start trading AXL Perpetual Futures now on PowerTrade or its decentralized counterpart Polaris.

What Are AXL Perpetual Futures?

AXL perpetual futures are leveraged derivatives that allow traders to take long or short positions on Axelar (AXL) without owning the underlying asset.

  • Long AXL Perpetual: Profit when AXL’s price rises.
  • Short AXL Perpetual: Profit when AXL’s price falls.

Unlike traditional dated futures, AXL perpetuals never expire. Instead, they use hourly funding payments between long and short traders to maintain alignment with AXL’s global spot market price.

AXL Perpetual Contract Specifications

Parameter Details
Instrument Name AXL-PERPETUAL
Underlying Asset Axelar (AXL)
Settlement Currency USDC
Leverage Up to 100×
Settlement Frequency Hourly
Trading Fees 0% maker / taker
Collateral Options BTC, ETH, and stablecoins
Margin Mode Isolated or Portfolio Margin

PowerTrade perpetuals combine zero-fee trading, multi-collateral flexibility, and institutional-grade performance, built for both professional and retail traders.

AXL Perpetual Funding Rate Explained

PowerTrade’s AXL funding rate ensures the perpetual contract price remains aligned with the AXL/USDC Index, sourced from CoinGecko.

Funding Rate Formula:

F = P + clamp(I – P, +0.05%, –0.05%)

Where:

  • P (Premium Index): Difference between market and index price.
  • I (Interest Rate): 0.01% per 8 hours (applied hourly).
  • Clamp: Restricts adjustments to ±0.05% to stabilize funding costs.

Funding is applied hourly, impacting only positions open at the settlement timestamp.
Positions closed beforehand incur no funding fees.

👉 Learn more: PowerTrade Funding Rate Calculation Guide

AXL/USDC Index Price Methodology

The AXL/USDC Index Price represents the fair global market value of Axelar (AXL) against USDC, calculated from aggregated spot market data through CoinGecko’s composite exchange feed.

📊 Reference Source: Axelar (AXL) on CoinGecko

Purpose of the Index

The index serves as the reference rate for AXL perpetual contracts — ensuring mark prices and liquidation levels mirror global AXL market conditions, not isolated exchange behavior.

This framework maintains pricing integrity, transparency, and fairness for all traders.

Index Construction

CoinGecko’s composite AXL index aggregates spot prices from top-tier exchanges and weights them by:

  • 24-hour trading volume and liquidity depth
  • Data reliability and uptime
  • Consistency with the global average

Outlier and stale data are automatically excluded.
PowerTrade integrates this validated feed to calculate mark prices and funding rates accurately.

Fallback Mechanism

If CoinGecko’s data feed becomes temporarily unavailable, PowerTrade switches to a pre-market internal index derived from AXL perpetual order-book bid/ask data.
This ensures continuous pricing and fair liquidations even during external data interruptions.

Mark Price and Liquidation Logic

PowerTrade’s mark price mechanism smooths volatility and ensures fair PnL and liquidation logic:

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price – Index Price)
  • The EMA (Exponential Moving Average) is calculated over a 15-second window to reduce short-term price noise.
  • This ensures fair liquidation levels and accurate mark-to-market accounting.

PnL Formulas:

  • Long AXL: PnL = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price)
  • Short AXL: PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidation Trigger:
Account Balance + Unrealized PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

Upon liquidation:

  1. Open orders are canceled.
  2. Positions are closed at market price.
  3. PowerTrade’s insurance fund and auto-deleveraging (ADL) systems maintain market stability.

Why Trade AXL Perpetual Futures on PowerTrade?

✅ 0% Trading Fees – Zero maker/taker fees across all perpetual markets.
✅ Hourly Funding – More frequent and transparent than 8-hour cycles.
✅ Benchmark-Aligned Pricing – CoinGecko AXL index ensures fairness.
✅ Multi-Collateral Margining – Use BTC, ETH, or stablecoins as collateral.
✅ Portfolio Margin – Cross-margin perpetuals and options for efficiency.
✅ Trade Anywhere – On PowerTrade (CEX) or Polaris (DEX).

PowerTrade’s AXL perpetual markets deliver deep liquidity, verifiable transparency, and institutional-grade performance — ideal for both active and professional traders.

Example AXL Perpetual Use Cases

1️⃣ Hedge AXL Spot Exposure
Short AXL perpetuals to protect your holdings during market pullbacks.

2️⃣ Leverage Trading
Amplify AXL exposure with leverage for directional opportunities.

3️⃣ Funding Rate Arbitrage
Profit from differences in AXL funding rates across exchanges.

4️⃣ Volatility Trading
Trade AXL’s intraday volatility using leverage and 0% trading fees.

Funding Transparency and Reproducibility

PowerTrade offers complete funding transparency — all rates are reproducible from public order-book and index data.
Every trader can independently verify calculations, reinforcing fairness and accountability.

👉 Full details: PowerTrade Perpetual Funding Rate Methodology

How to Start Trading AXL Perpetuals

  1. Create an account on PowerTrade or Polaris.
  2. Deposit collateral – USDC, BTC, or ETH.
  3. Select AXL-PERPETUAL from the perpetual markets tab.
  4. Trade instantly with 0% fees and hourly funding transparency.

Conclusion

PowerTrade’s AXL Perpetual Futures deliver the most efficient, transparent, and cost-effective way to trade Axelar with leverage.
Whether you’re hedging, speculating, or arbitraging, PowerTrade and Polaris offer the most advanced and liquid AXL perpetual markets in crypto.

What Are AXS Perpetual Futures?

AXS perpetual futures are leveraged derivatives that allow traders to take long or short positions on Axie Infinity (AXS) without owning the underlying token.

  • Long AXS Perpetual: Profit when AXS price rises.
  • Short AXS Perpetual: Profit when AXS price falls.

Unlike traditional futures, AXS perpetuals never expire. Instead, they use hourly funding payments exchanged between long and short traders to maintain alignment with AXS’s global spot price.

AXS Perpetual Contract Specifications

Parameter Details
Instrument Name AXS-PERPETUAL
Underlying Asset Axie Infinity (AXS)
Settlement Currency USDC
Leverage Up to 100×
Settlement Frequency Hourly
Trading Fees 0% maker / taker
Collateral Options BTC, ETH, and stablecoins
Margin Mode Isolated or Portfolio Margin

PowerTrade perpetuals combine zero-fee trading, multi-collateral margining, and benchmark-aligned transparency, designed for both institutional and retail traders.

AXS Perpetual Funding Rate Explained

PowerTrade’s AXS funding rate ensures perpetual contract prices stay aligned with the AXS/USDC index, derived from CoinGecko.

Funding Rate Formula:

F = P + clamp(I – P, +0.05%, –0.05%)

Where:

  • P (Premium Index): Difference between the market price and index price.
  • I (Interest Rate): 0.01% per 8 hours (applied hourly).
  • Clamp: Restricts adjustments to ±0.05% to prevent extreme funding volatility.

Funding is applied hourly and only affects positions open at settlement.
Close your position before funding time to avoid paying funding fees.

👉 Learn more: PowerTrade Funding Rate Calculation Guide

AXS/USDC Index Price Methodology

The AXS/USDC Index represents the fair global market value of Axie Infinity (AXS) against USDC, using aggregated spot data from CoinGecko’s composite index feed.

📊 Reference Source: Axie Infinity (AXS) on CoinGecko

Purpose of the Index

The index provides the reference rate for AXS perpetual contracts, ensuring mark prices and liquidation levels reflect the global market — not the movements of any single exchange.
This guarantees fairness, transparency, and price consistency.

Index Construction

CoinGecko’s composite index aggregates AXS/USDC (and equivalent) spot prices from top exchanges and weights them based on:

  • 24-hour trading volume and liquidity depth
  • Exchange reliability and uptime
  • Price consistency relative to the global average

Outlier and stale data are automatically removed.
PowerTrade integrates this verified CoinGecko feed to maintain transparent mark pricing.

Fallback Mechanism

If CoinGecko’s feed becomes unavailable, PowerTrade switches to an internal pre-market index based on bid/ask data from the AXS perpetual order book.
This ensures continuous and accurate mark pricing even during external data disruptions.

Mark Price and Liquidation Logic

PowerTrade uses a mark price mechanism that smooths volatility and ensures fair liquidation calculations:

Mark Price = Index Price + EMA(Market Price – Index Price)
  • The EMA (Exponential Moving Average) is calculated over a 15-second period to reduce short-term price noise.
  • This helps prevent unnecessary liquidations during volatile conditions.

PnL Formulas:

  • Long AXS: PnL = Position Size × (Mark Price – Entry Price)
  • Short AXS: PnL = Position Size × (Entry Price – Mark Price)

Liquidation Trigger:
Account Balance + Unrealized PnL ≤ Maintenance Margin

When triggered:

  1. Open orders are canceled.
  2. Positions are closed at market.
  3. The insurance fund and auto-deleveraging (ADL) system maintain platform stability.
